title: LiDAR Point Cloud 3D Mapping
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: LiDAR Point Cloud 3D Mapping: Airborne LiDAR mapping: recover 3D terrain/surface model from point cloud after boresight calibration. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.tof.range_equation…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; IMU_drift_deg, GPS_accuracy_cm set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: X_obj = R_IMU * R_boresight * (R_scan * r_vec + t_scan) + X_IMU
E.world_state_x: 3D surface elevation model DEM(x,y) or DSM
E.observation_y: point cloud (X,Y,Z,intensity) for N_points returns per flight strip

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D_point_cloud is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 20); IMU_drift_deg dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: vertical RMSE < 5 cm for airborne, < 2 cm for terrestrial. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~20. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |IMU_drift_deg|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_points * N_iter) for ICP registration per iteration
